prompt: Use the sheet-metal skill to produce all the deliverables for a top-down SolidWorks assembly that can be parametrically driven for different sizes. The first size is similar to a toolbox at around 20 in wide x 8 in tall x 10 in deep. The design should be stackable like modular toolbox systems and I want to include a dolly that can move three or four boxes around at a time.

assertions.text: Produces a design-brief.md with object, use case, envelope, and assumptions
Produces a parameters.csv with named master variables including thickness, bend radius, K-factor
Produces solidworks-equations.txt with quoted variable names usable in SolidWorks
Produces solidworks-design-table.csv with at least seed and one other configuration
Produces a solidworks-plan.md with master layout part strategy and feature order
Produces a stacking-interface description (clocking rim or cleats)
Produces a dolly-plan with thicker stock for the dolly chassis
Produces bend-table, BOM, hardware, cut-list, and load-cases CSVs
Marks authority level as design/planning, not fabrication-ready
Lists open measurements/decisions before fabrication release
